scores.materiality_reasoning: Loss of rights (atimia) represents a direct loss of legal personhood and material standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Citizenship is a foundational societal axis covering many outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of citizenship is described as a permanent removal from the state.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal birth-status, very hard to change or regain once lost.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes a law attributed to Solon, centuries before his time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites an enactment of law as an historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Neutrality in war is not part of the definition of a 'Citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from enslavement concerns bodily freedom and physical property status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Freedom from slavery is a foundational structural axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A ban on debt-slavery provides a lifelong structural protection.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizen status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the 6th-century BC reforms of Solon.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The text identifies this as one of three central democratic facts of the constitution.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently define one as immune to debt-bondage in all systems.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal standing to sue has direct material consequences for property and justice.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to claim redress covers a broad category of legal protections.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This legal right of standing is a permanent attribute of citizenship.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal/structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to Solonian reforms in the distant past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as one of the established 'points in the constitution'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The right to sue on behalf of others is a specific legal innovation, not a definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Appeal to courts is a concrete legal right that protects property and person.
scores.generality_reasoning: A major category of right spanning all magistrate decisions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The institution of appeal is a lifelong structural feature of the citizen's life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the historical constitution of Solon.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the institution of appeal as a factual historical development.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The right of appeal is a procedural right, not the definition of a citizen.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The Archonship is a high office with significant political power and material reach.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a single high office (Archonship).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is a lifelong structural attribute of the class.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Eupatridae are a hereditary noble lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a compromise from the early 6th century BC.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a formal agreed compromise as historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Eligibility for this specific office is not the definition of nobility.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The franchise (voting) is the primary engine of political power and rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: The franchise is a foundational societal right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of the citizen-roll is a permanent and lifelong change in status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by lineage/descent, which is strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a revision after the fall of the Peisistratids.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a historical event (revision of rolls) as proof of a claim.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lacking pure descent doesn't automatically mean 'disenfranchised' without this law.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct provision of money/capital for subsistence agriculture.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad category of access to capital for a large class.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loans have long-term effects on the viability of one's livelihood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The poor are a wealth bracket, changeable through effort or fortune.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the tyranny of Pisistratus (6th century BC).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific administrative practice of the tyrant.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being poor does not define one as a recipient of agricultural loans.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A tax is a direct material seizure of wealth/produce.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad tax affecting the entire class of producers.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Taxes on land/produce are lifelong obligations for owners.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Landownership is an economic role, though often stable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes historical taxation under Pisistratus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the imposition of a 10% tax as a neutral fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Landowners, by definition, are the targets of a produce/land tax; less harvest is implicit.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Local justice is a concrete legal resource with travel/time savings.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a particular judicial institution/reform.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Availability of local courts is a long-term structural feature.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Residence (rural vs city) is changeable but usually durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes historical reforms of Pisistratus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the institution of local justices as a historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Living in the country doesn't inherently imply having 'local' justices.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Total loss of civic rights (atimia) for the individual and family.
scores.generality_reasoning: Loss of all civic rights is a foundational punishment.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Sentence of atimia was generally permanent for the whole house.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by the act of attempting a tyranny; behavioral.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites 'ancestral statutes' from the archaic period.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim quotes the specific text of the statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: An aspiring tyrant is not, by definition, a person without rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Expulsion from territory is a total loss of residence and property access.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad penalty affecting residency and membership.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exile of families was usually perpetual or long-term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by lineage ('polluted families').
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the Cleisthenic period (late 6th century BC).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a massive historical expulsion as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being of a certain lineage doesn't define one as being in exile.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Official identity is a social role that grants access to legal/civic rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: The basis for all official identification and civic interaction.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Deme membership is a lifelong, hereditary structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship/Deme-status is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the historical reforms of Cleisthenes.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the renaming/reclassification policy as a factual change.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizens are not defined by 'location-based naming' by default.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Religious rites and clan heritage are mostly symbolic/immaterial.
scores.generality_reasoning: Relates to specific family and clan institutions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ancestral rites are lifelong and trans-generational.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Noble families and clans are immutable hereditary groups.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the outcome of Cleisthenes' reforms.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the retention of rights as a factual compromise.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Retention of rites is not part of the definition of a noble family.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Expulsion from the city is a material loss of land/home access.
scores.generality_reasoning: Loss of residency is a major structural outcome.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ostracism lasted 10 years; a large portion of an adult life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Powerful/influential citizens are a social bracket, often stable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the early 5th-century use of the law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports on the implementation of the law as an historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Powerful citizens are not defined by being ostracized.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Zonal restriction and permanent loss of rights are material penalties.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific restriction on movement for a subset of exiles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds for the duration of the 10-year ostracism period.
scores.immutability_reasoning: An episodic status (those under a specific sentence).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a 5th-century historical legal amendment.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the verbatim conditions of the decree as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Ostracized persons are not defined by the specific Geraestus boundary.

scores.materiality_reasoning: State-funded maintenance is direct financial income from tribute/taxes.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad system of income for thousands across various roles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Maintenance holds during long-term service (e.g., dockyard guards).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Role-bound (jurymen, guards) for specific terms.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the mid-5th century Athenian empire.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Provides detailed numbers to attest to the factual system.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Public service does not always definitionally include financial maintenance.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to high political office (Archonship) with material control.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the highest office but recurring.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is a lifelong structural right for members of that class.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Zeugitae are a census/property class; very stable structural status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a specific 5th-century legal decision.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the decision to open the lot as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The property-class definition doesn't include specific office eligibility.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The franchise provides the direct power to vote and hold status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal right for political participation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Determines lifelong status as a citizen or non-citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Mixed parentage is a strictly immutable fact of birth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes Pericles' citizenship law of 451 BC.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the motion and its historical context as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Mixed parentage doesn't automatically imply lack of franchise.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct cash payment (misthos) for jury service.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a single role (juror).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Pay is episodic, tied to active service sessions.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Episodic role based on selection for a trial.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the historical innovations of Pericles.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the institution of pay as a neutral historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of a juror does not inherently include being paid.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Power to frame constitutional measures for the state.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific, unique committee (Committee of Public Safety).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Brief episode during an emergency/reform period.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age (being under 40) is immutable at that moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the 411 BC oligarchic coup.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the specific rules of the Assembly's election decree.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being under forty does not define one as ineligible for all committees.
